enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 1These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
enriched_requirements.object: immediate effect

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to All-India Financial Institutions (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'AIFIs' and individually as 'an AIFI') viz.
enriched_requirements.object: Financial Institutions (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'AIFIs' and individually as 'an AIFI') viz

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In these directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Debtor company' means any company to which the regulated entity (RE)
currently has or previously had a loan or investment exposure (excluding
equity instruments) anytime during the preceding twelve months;
enriched_requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Debtor company' means any company to which the regulated entity (RE) currently has or previously had a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Equity instrument' means equity shares, compulsorily convertible
preference shares (CCPS) and compulsorily convertible debentures (CCD);
enriched_requirements.object: preference shares (CCPS) and compulsorily convertible debentures (CCD)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Capital funds' means the total regulatory capital of an AIFI, fulfilling the
criteria defined in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions
- Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025, as per the last
audited balance sheet;
enriched_requirements.object: of an AIFI, fulfilling the criteria defined in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Prudential N

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(4) 'Control' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under clause (27)
of Section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013 as amended from time to time;
enriched_requirements.object: as assigned to it under clause (27) of Section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013 as amended from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) 'Financial services company' means a company engaged in the 'business
of financial services'.
enriched_requirements.object: the 'business of financial services'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Explanation: The 'business of financial services' shall include -

The following instructions shall be read with the extant prudential norms on
Exposures for the AIFIs.
enriched_requirements.object: extant prudential norms on Exposures for the AIFIs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Prior approval of the RBI shall not be required if the
investments in the financial services companies are held under the
"Held for Trading" category as stipulated in the Reserve Bank of India
(All India Financial Institutions - Classification, Valuation and
Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025, as updated from
time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: investments in the financial services companies are held under the "Held for Trading" category as stipulated in the Reserve Bank of

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Provided that, no AIFI shall make an investment in a Category III AIF.
enriched_requirements.object: a Category III AIF

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Investment by an AIFI's subsidiary in a Category III AIF shall be restricted
to the regulatory minima prescribed by SEBI.
enriched_requirements.object: regulatory minima prescribed by SEBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Note: AIFIs shall ascertain the risks arising on account of equity
investments in Alternative Investment Funds done directly or through their
subsidiaries, within the Internal Capital Adequacy Assessment Process
(ICAAP) framework and determine the additional capital required which will
be subject to supervisory examination as part of Supervisory Review and
Evaluation Process.
enriched_requirements.object: on account of equity investments in Alternative Investment Funds done directly or through their subsidiaries, within the I

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
AIFIs shall formulate policies, with the approval of their Board, covering the
following aspects of investments in financial entities to the extent these are not
covered under the cross-holding limits prescribed under the Large Exposure
Norms, under Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions -
Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: approval of their Board, covering the following aspects of investments in financial entities to the extent these are not

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
AIFIs shall adhere to the following limits in terms of percentage of the paid-up
capital and reserves of the AIFI as per the last audited balance sheet or a
subsequent balance sheet, whichever is lower,
(1) Investment in equity of a single financial services entity which is not an
affiliate of the AIFI;
enriched_requirements.object: limits in terms of percentage of the paid-up capital and reserves of the AIFI as per the last audited balance sheet or a subse

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
No AIFI shall, without the prior approval of the RBI, make:
(1) Investment in a subsidiary and a financial services company that is not a
subsidiary.
enriched_requirements.object: approval of the RBI, make: (1) Investment in a subsidiary and a financial services company that is not a subsidiary

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, such prior approval shall not be necessary in the following
circumstances:
<image>
enriched_requirements.object: following circumstances: <image>

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
AIFIs shall strictly adhere to the limits, both at an individual and aggregate level,
on investments in subsidiaries, financial and non-financial entities prescribed
under Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Prudential Norms
on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025 read with Reserve Bank of India (All India
Financial Institutions - Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: limits, both at an individual and aggregate level, on investments in subsidiaries, financial and non-financial entities prescri

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI's investment policy shall have suitable provisions governing its
investments in an AIF Scheme, compliant with extant law and regulations.
enriched_requirements.object: its investments in an AIF Scheme, compliant with extant law and regulations

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall not individually contribute more than 10 percent of the corpus of an
AIF Scheme.
enriched_requirements.object: 10 percent of the corpus of an AIF Scheme

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate contribution by all Regulated Entities (REs) in any AIF Scheme
shall not be more than 20 percent of the corpus of that scheme.
enriched_requirements.object: percent of the corpus of that scheme

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, 'RE' shall mean:
(1) Commercial Banks (including Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks);
enriched_requirements.object: (including Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks)

conditions:
IF Scheme
that has downstream investment (excluding equity instruments) in a debtor
company of the AIFI
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where an AIFI contributes more than five percent of the corpus of an AIF Scheme
that has downstream investment (excluding equity instruments) in a debtor
company of the AIFI, the AIFI shall be required to make 100 percent provision to
the extent of its proportionate investment in the debtor company through the AIF
Scheme, subject to a cap equivalent to AIFI's direct loan and / or investment
exposure to the said debtor company.
enriched_requirements.object: 100 percent provision to the extent of its proportionate investment in the debtor company through the AIF Scheme

conditions:
where an AIFI's
contribution is in the form of subordinated units
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph 14 above, where an AIFI's
contribution is in the form of subordinated units, it shall deduct the entire
investment from its capital funds ‒ proportionately from both Tier-1 and Tier-2
capital (wherever applicable).
enriched_requirements.object: from its capital funds ‒ proportionately from both Tier-1 and Tier-2 capital (wherever applicable)

conditions: IF contained in Part A.3 of Chapter II
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
3The RBI may, in consultation with the Government of India, by way of a
notification, exempt certain AIFs (Annex-I) from the scope of these Directions on
AIF contained in Part A.3 of Chapter II, except for paragraph 11 above.
enriched_requirements.object: the Government of India, by way of a notification, exempt certain AIFs (Annex-I) from the scope of these Directions on AIF contained in Part A.3 of Chapter II

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI desirous of making an investment or undertaking any business that
requires prior approval / no-objection of RBI shall submit an application through
PRAVAAH Portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in) to the Department of Regulation
(DoR), Central Office, RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: PRAVAAH Portal (https://pravaah

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A parent / sponsor AIFI shall maintain an 'arm's length' relationship with the
subsidiary sponsored by it and evolve the following supervisory strategies:
(1) The Board of the parent / sponsor AIFI shall review the working of
subsidiaries at periodical intervals.
enriched_requirements.object: relationship with the subsidiary sponsored by it and evolve the following supervisory strategies: (1) The Board of the pare

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The parent / sponsor AIFI shall undertake inspection/audit of the books of
accounts of the subsidiaries at periodical intervals.
enriched_requirements.object: books of accounts of the subsidiaries at periodical intervals

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The subsidiary shall not set up another subsidiary or promote a new
company which is not a subsidiary thereof, or undertake any new business
without prior approval of the RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: or promote a new company which is not a subsidiary thereof, or undertake any new business without prior approval of the RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: 'New Business' shall not mean expansion into the same line of
business that is already permitted/approved to be undertaken.
enriched_requirements.object: same line of business that is already permitted/approved to be undertaken

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The subsidiary shall not make any portfolio investment in another existing
company with an intention of acquiring controlling interest, without prior
approval of the RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: in another existing company with an intention of acquiring controlling interest, without prior approval of the RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This shall not apply to the investments made by a
Category I and II AIF set up by a subsidiary.
enriched_requirements.object: made by a Category I and II AIF set up by a subsidiary

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) A subsidiary shall not have any on-line access to customers' accounts
maintained with the AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: to customers' accounts maintained with the AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The information between an AIFI and its subsidiary
may be shared subject to maintaining arm's length relationship.
enriched_requirements.object: maintaining arm's length relationship

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The AIFI shall not grant any unsecured advances to the subsidiary without
prior approval of the RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: to the subsidiary without prior approval of the RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (7) Transactions between an AIFI and its subsidiary shall be at arm's length.

No preferential treatment shall be given to the subsidiary vis-à-vis a
counterparty with similar risk characteristics.
enriched_requirements.object: subsidiary vis-à-vis a counterparty with similar risk characteristics

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI, including those operating in International Financial Services Centers
(IFSCs) in India such as Gujarat International Finance Tec City (GIFT City), shall
comply with the following directions regarding dealing in financial derivative
products by its foreign branches / subsidiaries.
enriched_requirements.object: directions regarding dealing in financial derivative products by its foreign branches / subsidiaries

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the branches / subsidiaries, if any, of AIFIs operating in IFSCs including
those operating out of GIFT City may also deal in financial derivative products,
including structured financial derivative products, which are not available or are
not permitted by the RBI in the domestic market subject to compliance with all
applicable laws / regulations and conditions stipulated in paragraph 25 below and
those prescribed by the host regulator.
enriched_requirements.object: derivative products, including structured financial derivative products, which are not available or are not permitted by the RB

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While allowing branches / subsidiaries in foreign jurisdictions as well as in IFSCs
to deal in such products, the parent AIFI shall ensure that:
(1) dealing in such products is done with the prior approval from their Board
and, if required, the appropriate authority in the concerned jurisdictions;
enriched_requirements.object: in such products is done with the prior approval from their Board and

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
They shall provide information about dealing in such financial products as
<image>
may be specified by the RBI in the manner and format and within the time
frame as prescribed by the RBI;
enriched_requirements.object: in such financial products as <image> may be specified by the RBI in the manner and format and within the time frame

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The financial products dealt with by the foreign branches and subsidiaries as well
as IFSCs shall attract the prudential norms such as capital adequacy, exposure
norms (including Large Exposure Framework), periodical valuation, and all other
applicable norms.
enriched_requirements.object: such as capital adequacy, exposure norms (including Large Exposure Framework), periodical valuation, and all other applic

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
AIFI shall adhere to more stringent among the host and home
regulations in respect of prudential norms.
enriched_requirements.object: among the host and home regulations in respect of prudential norms

conditions:
In case the current norms of the RBI do not specify prudential treatment of any
financial product
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case the current norms of the RBI do not specify prudential treatment of any
financial product, the AIFI shall seek specific guidance from RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The activities of branches / subsidiaries in foreign jurisdictions and IFSCs shall
be subject to the laws in India, unless specifically exempted by law.
enriched_requirements.object: laws in India

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may
be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or
punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or
guidelines had not been repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
enriched_requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
<image>
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of
any provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
